Problem Statement: Multiply Polynomial Functions
Question: For functions $$f ( x ) = x + 2$$ and $$g ( x ) = 3 x^{2} - 2 x + 4 ,$$ find ⓐ $$( f \cdotp g ) ( x )$$ ⓑ $$( f \cdotp g ) ( -1 )$$

Problem Statement: Multiply Polynomial Functions
Question: For functions $$f ( x ) = x - 1$$ and $$g ( x ) = 4 x^{2} + 3 x - 5 ,$$ find ⓐ $$( f \cdotp g ) ( x )$$ ⓑ $$( f \cdotp g ) ( -2 )$$

Problem Statement: Multiply Polynomial Functions
Question: For functions $$f ( x ) = 2 x - 7$$ and $$g ( x ) = 2 x + 7 ,$$ find ⓐ $$( f \cdotp g ) ( x )$$ ⓑ $$( f \cdotp g ) ( -3 )$$

Problem Statement: Multiply Polynomial Functions

Problem Statement: Multiply Polynomial Functions

Problem Statement: Multiply Polynomial Functions
Question: For functions $$f ( x ) = x^{2} + 4 x - 3$$ and $$g ( x ) = x^{2} + 2 x + 4 ,$$ find ⓐ $$( f \cdotp g ) ( x )$$ ⓑ $$( f \cdotp g ) ( 1 )$$

Which method do you prefer to use when multiplying two binomials: the Distributive Property or the FOIL method? Why? Which method do you prefer to use when multiplying a polynomial by a polynomial: the Distributive Property or the Vertical Method? Why?
Multiply the following: $$\left .\begin{matrix}{a}( x + 2 ) ( x - 2 ) \\ ( y + 7 ) ( y - 7 ) \\ ( w + 5 ) ( w - 5 )\end{matrix}\right .$$ Explain the pattern that you see in your answers.
Multiply the following: $$\left .\begin{matrix}{a}( p + 3 ) ( p + 3 ) \\ ( q + 6 ) ( q + 6 ) \\ ( r + 1 ) ( r + 1 )\end{matrix}\right .$$ Explain the pattern that you see in your answers.
Why does $$( a + b )^{2}$$ result in a trinomial, but $$( a - b ) ( a + b )$$ result in a binomial?
